title: हनुमान ध्यानम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री हनुमान ध्यानम् ॥

अतुलितबलधामं हेमशैलाभदेहं
दनुजवनकृशानुं ज्ञानिनामग्रगण्यम्।
सकलगुणनिधानं वानराणामधीशं
रघुपतिप्रियभक्तं वातजातं नमामि॥ १॥

मनोजवं मारुततुल्यवेगं
जितेन्द्रियं बुद्धिमतां वरिष्ठम्।
वातात्मजं वानरयूथमुख्यं
श्रीरामदूतं शरणं प्रपद्ये॥ २॥

बुद्धिर्बलं यशो धैर्यं निर्भयत्वमरोगता।
अजाड्यं वाक्पटुत्वं च हनूमत्स्मरणाद्भवेत्॥ ३॥

गोष्पदीकृतवारीशं मशकीकृतराक्षसम्।
रामायणमहामालारत्नं वन्देऽनिलात्मजम्॥ ४॥

उल्लङ्घ्य सिन्धोः सलिलं सलीलं
यः शोकवह्निं जनकात्मजायाः।
आदाय तेनैव ददाह लङ्कां
नमामि तं प्राञ्जलिराञ्जनेयम्॥ ५॥

आञ्जनेयं अतिपाटलाननं
काञ्चनाद्रिकमनीयविग्रहम्।
पारिजाततरुमूलवासिनं
भावयामि पवमाननन्दनम्॥ ६॥

यत्र यत्र रघुनाथकीर्तनं
तत्र तत्र कृतमस्तकाञ्जलिम्।
बाष्पवारिपरिपूर्णलोचनं
मारुतिं नमत राक्षसान्तकम्॥ ७॥

ॐ हनुमते नमः।
ॐ आञ्जनेयाय विद्महे वायुपुत्राय धीमहि।
तन्नो हनुमत् प्रचोदयात्॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री हनुमान ध्यानम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Hanuman Dhyanam ||

Atulita Bala Dhamam Hema Shailabha Deham
Danuja Vana Krishanum Jnaninam Agraganyam |
Sakala Guna Nidhanam Vanaranam Adhisham
Raghupati Priya Bhaktam Vatajatam Namami || 1 ||

Manojavam Maruta Tulya Vegam
Jitendriyam Buddhimatam Varishtham |
Vatatmajam Vanara Yutha Mukhyam
Shriramadutam Sharanam Prapadye || 2 ||

Buddhir Balam Yasho Dhairyam Nirbhayatvam Arogata |
Ajadyam Vakpatutavam Cha Hanumatsmaranad Bhavet || 3 ||

Goshpadikrita Varisham Mashakikrita Rakshasam |
Ramayana Mahamala Ratnam Vande'nilatmajam || 4 ||

Ullanghya Sindhoh Salilam Salilam
Yah Shokavahnim Janakatmajayah |
Adaya Tenaiva Dadaha Lankam
Namami Tam Pranjalir Anjaneyam || 5 ||

Anjaneyam Atipatala Ananam
Kanchana Adri Kamaniya Vigraham |
Parijata Taru Mula Vasinam
Bhavayami Pavamana Nandanam || 6 ||

Yatra Yatra Raghunatha Kirtanam
Tatra Tatra Krita Mastaka Anjalim |
Bashpa Vari Paripurna Lochanam
Marutim Namata Rakshasantakam || 7 ||

Om Hanumate Namah |
Om Anjaneyaya Vidmahe Vayuputraya Dhimahi |
Tanno Hanumat Prachodayat || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Hanuman Dhyanam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
अतुलित बलधाम, स्वर्ण पर्वत सदृश शरीर वाले, दैत्यवन के अग्नि, ज्ञानियों में अग्रगण्य, सकलगुणनिधान, वानरों के अधीश, रघुपति के प्रिय भक्त, वायुपुत्र को नमस्कार।

मन के समान वेगवान, मारुत के तुल्य गतिशील, जितेन्द्रिय, बुद्धिमानों में श्रेष्ठ, वायुपुत्र, वानरयूथप्रमुख, श्रीरामदूत की शरण लेता हूँ।

हनुमान के स्मरण से बुद्धि, बल, यश, धैर्य, निर्भयता, आरोग्य, स्फूर्ति और वाक्पटुता प्राप्त होती है।

समुद्र को गोखुर सदृश (तुच्छ) बनाने वाले, राक्षसों को मच्छर सदृश करने वाले, रामायण की महामाला के रत्न वायुपुत्र को वन्दन।

समुद्र का जल सहज ही लांघकर, सीता के शोकाग्नि को लेकर उसी से लंका जलाने वाले आञ्जनेय को हाथ जोड़कर नमस्कार।

जहाँ-जहाँ रघुनाथ का कीर्तन होता है, वहाँ-वहाँ हाथ जोड़े, आँसुओं से भरे नेत्रों वाले, राक्षसान्तक मारुति को नमन करो।
benefits: Complete Hanuman meditation collection with the most beloved dhyana shlokas
Contains Atulita Baladhamam, Manojavam and Yatra Yatra verses
Hanuman smarana grants 8 divine qualities: wisdom, strength, fame, courage, fearlessness, health, vigor, eloquence
Includes Hanuman Gayatri mantra for deeper meditation
Ideal for daily morning meditation and Hanuman puja
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Hanuman, Hanuman Dhyanam (हनुमान ध्यानम्) stands as a sacred prayer. Rooted in Hanuman Chalisa Tradition / Valmiki Ramayana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to contain Atulita Baladhamam, Manojavam and Yatra Yatra verses as well as hanuman smarana grants 8 divine qualities: wisdom, strength, fame, courage, fearlessness, health, vigor, eloquence. Additionally, it is known to complete Hanuman meditation collection with the most beloved dhyana shlokas. This prayer is traditionally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar), Saturday (Shanivar), and Daily and during Hanuman Jayanti to invoke divine grace. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Hanuman Dhyanam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Hanuman Dhyanam and what does it contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hanuman Dhyanam (हनुमान ध्यानम्) is a curated collection of the most beloved and powerful dhyana shlokas (meditation verses) on Lord Hanuman, gathered from the Hanuman Chalisa tradition, Valmiki Ramayana, Ramcharitmanas and the wider Hanuman stotra corpus. The eight-verse compilation includes the universally-recited Atulita-baladhamam shloka, the Manojavam-maruta-tulya-vegam (the Sundara Kanda dhyana shloka), the celebrated Buddhir-balam-yasho-dhairyam (which enumerates the eight divine qualities granted by Hanuman-smarana), the Goshpadi-krita-varisham, the Ullanghya-sindhoh-salilam (recalling the leap to Lanka), the Anjaneyam-atipataala-ananam, the famous Yatra-yatra-Raghunatha-kirtanam (Hanuman is wherever Rama's name is sung), and concluding with the Hanuman Gayatri — Om Anjaneyaya Vidmahe Vayu-putraya Dhimahi tanno Hanumat Prachodayat. (Sources: Hanuman Chalisa tradition; Valmiki Ramayana Sundara Kanda; Hanuman stotra parampara.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of 'Atulita Baladhamam Hema Shailabha Deham'?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse — Atulita-baladhamam Hema-Shailabha-deham, Danuja-vana-krishanum jnaninam-agraganyam, Sakala-guna-nidhanam Vanaranam-adhisham, Raghupati-priya-bhaktam Vatajatam Namami — is one of the most widely-recited Hanuman dhyana shlokas, especially as the invocation before Hanuman Chalisa parayana. Word-meaning: Atulita-baladhamam — 'abode of incomparable strength'; Hema-Shailabha-deham — 'whose body shines like the golden mountain (Meru/Sumeru)'; Danuja-vana-krishanum — 'the very fire to the forest of demons'; jnaninam agraganyam — 'foremost among the wise'; Sakala-guna-nidhanam — 'treasury of all noble qualities'; Vanaranam-adhisham — 'lord of the vanaras'; Raghupati-priya-bhaktam — 'the dearest devotee of Raghupati Rama'; Vatajatam — 'son of Vayu'; Namami — 'I bow.' (Sources: Hanuman Chalisa invocatory tradition; Goswami Tulsidas.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the 'Manojavam Maruta tulya vegam' shloka invoke?
seoFaqs.answer: The shloka — Manojavam Maruta-tulya-vegam, jitendriyam buddhimatam varishtham, Vatatmajam vanara-yutha-mukhyam, Shri-rama-dutam sharanam prapadye — is the celebrated dhyana invocation from the opening of Sundara Kanda of Valmiki Ramayana, recited just before the great leap to Lanka. Word-meaning: Manojavam — 'as swift as the mind'; Maruta-tulya-vegam — 'whose speed equals the wind'; jitendriyam — 'master of the senses'; buddhimatam varishtham — 'foremost among the wise'; Vatatmajam — 'son of the Wind'; vanara-yutha-mukhyam — 'leader of the vanara host'; Shri-rama-dutam — 'the messenger of Sri Rama'; sharanam prapadye — 'I take refuge.' This single shloka is recited at the beginning of every Sundara Kanda parayana and is one of the most commonly recommended verses for Tuesday and Saturday evening worship. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana Sundara Kanda; Sundara Kanda parayana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the eight divine qualities granted by Hanuman-smarana?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 3 — Buddhir balam yasho dhairyam nirbhayatvam-arogata, ajadyam vakpatutavam cha Hanumat-smaranad bhavet — enumerates the eight ashtaka-sampada (eight divine qualities) that arise simply from the constant smarana (remembrance) of Lord Hanuman. They are: (1) Buddhi — sharp intellect and discrimination; (2) Bala — physical and mental strength; (3) Yasha — fame and reputation; (4) Dhairya — courage and unshakeable steadiness; (5) Nirbhayatva — fearlessness; (6) Arogata — perfect health and freedom from disease; (7) Ajadya — alertness, freedom from inertia, vigour; (8) Vakpatutva — eloquence, fluency in speech. This single verse summarises the entire phala-shruti of Hanuman upasana and is recited by students before exams, by speakers before public addresses, and by anyone facing fear, illness or weakness. (Sources: Hanuman Dhyanam verse 3; Valmiki Ramayana attribution.)

seoFaqs.question: What does 'Yatra yatra Raghunatha kirtanam' teach about Hanuman's presence?
seoFaqs.answer: The beautiful verse — Yatra yatra Raghunatha-kirtanam, tatra tatra krita-mastaka-anjalim, bashpa-vari-paripurna-lochanam, Marutim namata rakshasantakam — declares one of the most cherished truths of Hanuman bhakti. Yatra yatra Raghunatha-kirtanam — 'wherever the kirtan of Raghunatha (Sri Rama) takes place'; tatra tatra — 'right there'; krita-mastaka-anjalim — 'with hands joined above the head in salutation'; bashpa-vari-paripurna-lochanam — 'with eyes brimming with tears of devotion'; Marutim namata rakshasantakam — 'salute Maruti, the destroyer of demons.' The verse promises that wherever Lord Rama's name is sung — in any home, temple, satsang or hridaya — Hanuman is invisibly present, head bowed, hands folded, eyes overflowing with tears of bhakti. This is the supreme assurance of every Rama-bhakta. (Sources: Hanuman Dhyanam verse 7; Goswami Tulsidas tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Hanuman Gayatri included in this collection?
seoFaqs.answer: The collection concludes with the Hanuman Gayatri — Om Anjaneyaya Vidmahe Vayu-putraya Dhimahi, tanno Hanumat Prachodayat — preceded by the bija namaskara Om Hanumate Namah. Word-meaning: Om Anjaneyaya Vidmahe — 'we know (meditate upon) the son of Anjana'; Vayu-putraya Dhimahi — 'we contemplate the son of Vayu'; tanno Hanumat Prachodayat — 'may that Hanuman illumine and inspire us.' This Gayatri is one of the deva-Gayatris of the classical 24-Gayatri set and is recited 108 times daily by Hanuman upasakas, especially on Tuesdays and Saturdays. It uniquely identifies Hanuman through both his maternal name (Anjaneya — son of Anjana) and his paternal name (Vayu-putra — son of the Wind). (Sources: Deva-Gayatri tradition; Hanuman upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Hanuman Dhyanam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Hanuman Dhyanam is most powerfully recited on Tuesdays (Mangalvar) and Saturdays (Shanivar) — Hanuman's two special days — and is also recommended for daily morning sadhana. It is especially auspicious on Hanuman Jayanti (the most widely observed date is Chaitra Purnima, with regional variants on Margashirsha Krishna Chaturdashi in some traditions); throughout the months of Chaitra and Shravan; on every Pradosh; on the day of any new venture, journey, exam or challenge; before beginning Sundara Kanda parayana; and before Hanuman Chalisa parayana as the invocatory dhyana. The recitation should be done at Brahma muhurta after bath, before an image of Hanuman with a sindoor-tilak, with offerings of red flowers, jasmine oil lamp and gud-chana prasad. (Sources: Hanuman upasana paddhati; Tuesday-Saturday vrata tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Hanuman Dhyanam?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Hanuman acharyas attribute extensive benefits to sincere Hanuman Dhyanam recitation: the eight ashtaka-sampada explicitly named in verse 3 — buddhi, bala, yasha, dhairya, nirbhayatva, arogata, ajadya and vakpatutva (intellect, strength, fame, courage, fearlessness, health, vigour and eloquence); protection from all kinds of bhaya — physical, mental, supernatural and political; freedom from grahapida (especially Shani and Mangal-dosha, since Hanuman is the controller of grahas); cultivation of unshakeable Rama-bhakti; success in challenging situations equivalent to Hanuman's own leap across the ocean (Ullanghya sindhoh salilam); the constant invisible presence of Maruti during Rama-naam-kirtana; protection of children, elders and travellers; relief from chronic illness when paired with Sankata-mochana japa; and ultimately the supreme phala — Sri Rama's grace through Hanuman, who is His foremost devotee. (Sources: Hanuman Dhyanam Phalashruti; Sundara Kanda parayana tradition.)
